metadata.title:
HARTMAN HILL
FIGHT ON THE


COAST

pages.raw_text:
The withdrawal of the engineers
of the Union Pacific working be-
tween aeSttle and the Columbia riv-
er has created CONSERVATION among
real estate men on the Sound. 1t
was rumored that the Union Pacific
would not build to Seattle but would
use the Milwaukee terminals. The
reason given by railroad men for
the sudden withdrawal of the engi-
neers is that the fight between Har-
riman and Hill has been transferred
to the proposed liAe from Van
couver along the north bank to the
sea and north to Aberdeen. This
will be an extension of the north
bank line Hill is building from Ken
newick to Vancouver.


1t has been denied that this ex
tension Is proposed, but j. c. Bar
torn, formerly of the Union Pacific
is now surveying the route for the
Northern Pacific and Great North
ern. He is the authority for the
statement that Union Pacific engi
neers have lately been sent into the
district to combat his locations and
to survey a competing route for the
Hartman system. The matter OF
route there depends to some extent
on first locations. just as it did
along the upper bank of the Co
lumbia.
